mnemonics: FCDP: First Comes, First Departs (FIFO), Circular for reuse, Double-ended for both ends, Priority for importance
keyPoints: A Simple Queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle.
A Circular Queue reuses the empty spaces created after dequeuing to optimize memory.
A Priority Queue dequeues elements based on their priority, not their arrival order.
commonMistakes: Assuming a full Circular Queue when the front pointer is just ahead of the rear, which is a normal state.
Treating a Priority Queue like a simple FIFO queue and being surprised by the output order.
Forgetting to reset pointers to the start of the array in a Circular Queue, leading to out-of-bounds errors.
